mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in Industrial Production (INDPRO) captures real-time manufacturing and utilities momentum; higher growth predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns because it signals expanding output that supports corporate revenues.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns do not occur in >55% of months when INDPRO growth is positive, or Rank IC < 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals a deteriorating labor market that reduces household income and consumption, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:April 2020 vintage UNRATE at 4.4% with payrolls collapsing
failure_condition: When triggered, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in >50% of instances, or the mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in Industrial Production (INDPRO) and Retail Sales (RSAFS) indicate a synchronized supply-demand contraction that is not captured by either series alone; this joint deterioration predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 60-day returns do not occur in >50% of cases when both INDPRO and RSAFS decline month-over-month, or the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power (F-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During periods when the federal funds rate is below 1%, month-over-month increases in the Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL) predict positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days because reflation expectations boost nominal earnings and reduce real debt burdens in a low-rate environment.
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle with FEDFUNDS below 1%
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in <55% of instances when CPIAUCSL rises while FEDFUNDS < 1%, or the Sharpe ratio of the signal is negative over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month changes in Housing Starts (HOUST) lead S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days because residential investment drives cyclical demand for construction, materials, and durable goods; positive growth predicts positive forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC between HOUST changes and 60-day forward returns is below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) exceeds 4.5%, month-over-month declines in Industrial Production (INDPRO) predict negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days because tight monetary policy restricts credit and amplifies the negative impact of falling output on corporate profits.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and INDPRO declines, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in >50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP (GDPC1) predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days because it captures the broadest measure of domestic economic activity; positive growth signals aggregate demand sufficient to support top-line revenue expansion.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns do not occur in >55% of quarters when GDPC1 growth is positive, or Rank IC < 0.02 over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in the Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL) and the unemployment rate (UNRATE) form a stagflationary signal that compresses profit margins and raises discount rates; this interaction predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns do not occur in >50% of months when both CPIAUCSL and UNRATE rise month-over-month, or the interaction term does not significantly improve prediction over main effects (likelihood ratio p > 0.05).
